decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize the research record as vLLM-positive; revisit SGLang only after an SM121-compatible SGLang/Triton build is available.
decision.stop_reason: Existing durable vLLM quality runs show DFlash beating the +15% throughput threshold at equal measured quality on four prompt sets with zero request failures, so further same-mechanism work would be churn.

recommended_next_action: Finalize the research record as vLLM-positive; revisit SGLang only after an SM121-compatible SGLang/Triton build is available.
run_notes_tail: 4. Stop helper servers, summarize durable artifacts and scientific decision evidence in this file, then update `.omx/project_decision.json`.
## 2026-04-13 quality-scored vLLM results

- Added `scripts/quality_endpoint_eval.py`, a quality-aware OpenAI-compatible evaluator that records completions, token throughput, p50/p95 latency, request failures, GPU samples, Prometheus metrics, and task quality. Scoring used exact/numeric extraction for GSM8K and MATH-500, subprocess unit tests for a local MBPP-style function-test set (HF MBPP loading hung in this environment, so the local set is the durable code gate unless `MBPP_USE_HF=1` is set), and non-empty/stability checks for chat prompts.

- Added `scripts/run_vllm_quality_matrix.sh`, which launches one vLLM server at a time from `/tmp/dflash-vllm-services` with equal Qwen/Qwen3-4B settings: `flash_attn`, `--max-num-batched-tokens 8192`, `max_new_tokens=256`, concurrency 4. DFlash only adds `--speculative-config {method:dflash, model:z-lab/Qwen3-4B-DFlash-b16, num_speculative_tokens:16}`.

- Baseline completed GSM8K (64), MATH-500 (40), MBPP-local (24), and chat (32) with 0 request failures. The first DFlash quality launch failed because a stray `.venv-vllm/bin/python -` process from a prior dataset probe left GPU memory unavailable (`Free memory ... 4.81/121.63 GiB`); killed that orphan and reran DFlash successfully. No vLLM/SGLang helper server remained after completion.
| dataset | n | baseline quality | DFlash quality | quality delta | baseline tok/s | DFlash tok/s | speedup | baseline p95 s | DFlash p95 s |

- DFlash met the kill-condition throughput criterion at equal measured quality on all four evaluated prompt sets: +60.6% chat, +204.1% GSM8K, +316.6% MATH-500, and +295.5% MBPP-local, each with 0 failed requests for both baseline and DFlash.

- DFlash Prometheus metrics showed active speculative decoding during quality runs. The final DFlash chat summary contained cumulative `spec_decode_num_drafts_total=5859`, `spec_decode_num_draft_tokens_total=93744`, and `spec_decode_num_accepted_tokens_total=19921`.

- Durable artifacts: `results/vllm_qwen3_4b_quality_comparison.json`, per-run `results/vllm_*_quality_*.summary.json`, `.samples.csv`, `.items.json`, `.gpu.csv`, server logs under `logs/vllm_*_server_quality_*.log`, and command JSON under `artifacts/vllm_*_commands_quality_*.json`.

- Scientific decision: finalize positive for the vLLM serving-stack hypothesis. SGLang remains separately parked on GB10 SM121 compatibility based on earlier concrete failures (`trtllm_mha` SM100-only, `fa4` RMSNorm no kernel image, `torch_native` Triton/ptxas `sm_121a` unsupported), but the same-backend vLLM evidence is now strong enough to stop this project without successor churn.
## 2026-04-14 controller review / finalization check

1. Reviewed the current project directory before doing more expensive work; it already contains isolated vLLM/SGLang envs, the upstream DFlash clone, benchmark harness scripts, server logs, command artifacts, mini-matrix results, and quality-scored vLLM comparison results.

2. Re-checked the durable comparison artifacts instead of launching another benchmark run, because the prior quality matrix already satisfies the kill-condition inverse: DFlash exceeded +15% throughput at equal measured quality on more than two datasets.
3. Verified no vLLM/SGLang/DFlash helper server processes were still running from this project; `nvidia-smi` showed 0% GPU utilization at review time.

4. Decision remains `finalize_positive`: continue would be churn on the same mechanism, and SGLang follow-up should wait for an SM121-compatible SGLang/Triton build rather than creating a generic successor branch.
### 2026-04-14 evidence recap
- Quality-scored vLLM runs at concurrency 4 showed zero request failures for both baseline and DFlash across chat, GSM8K, MATH-500, and MBPP-local.
- Equal-quality DFlash throughput speedups from `results/vllm_qwen3_4b_quality_comparison.json`: chat 1.61x, GSM8K 3.04x, MATH-500 4.17x, MBPP-local 3.96x.
- Mini-matrix smoke runs also showed DFlash above +15% in every chat/GSM8K-smoke concurrency cell (1/4/8), with active vLLM speculative decoding counters captured in summaries.
- SGLang was not treated as unexplored: documented GB10 failures include `trtllm_mha` SM100-only, `fa4` RMSNorm no-kernel-image, and `torch_native` Triton/ptxas `sm_121a` unsupported.
4. Stop helper servers, summarize durable artifacts and scientific decision evidence in this file, then update `.omx/project_decision.json`.
